Stcte of Cclifornlc
Memorondum
To . KATHRYN DONOVAN
ceneral Counsel
Fair Politicaf Practices
428 J Street, Suite 4L0
FPPC
JAN 2 9 $90
RI. 
-D
Commiss ion
Dote . January 31, 1990
File No.:
Subiect: CONFLICT OF INTERESTS
From : f,ffiSer$flo8F.+grftions
L107 - 9th Street, 8th Floor
Sacramento, CA 95814
on behalf of Christine w. Bender, Commissioner of corporations
( "Commissionerrr) , I am requesting an informal advice fetter fron
the staff of the Fair PoIitical Practices Commission with respect
to any possible violations of the potitical Reform Act resul-ting
from the hiring of an independent, outside counsel to be paid by
the Departrnent of Corporations ('rDepartmenttt) in connection with
pending litigation 
resulting from the Commissioner's approval of
a public debt offering of subordinated debentures issued by
American Continental Corporation (ttAcctt). Due to the urgency of
this matter, I request an early opinion and. am avai]able to
respond to any request for additional information necessary to
receive your informal advice l-etter.
The Commissioner is the head of the Department, which administers
numerous 1aws, including the Corporate Securities Law of 1969
(corporations Code Sections 25OOO et ggg., rCSLrt), the Franchise
Investment Law, the Knox-Keene Health Care Service plan Act of
1975, the california credit Union Law, the Industrial Loan Laur,
the Consumer and Commercial, Lending Laws, and the Escrow Law.
FACTS
fn 1988, the Comnissioner, after consulting with attorneys and
examiners on her staff, allowed to go effective one application
for qualification by coordination (with the federat Selurities
and Exchange commission) filed under Section 25111- of the cSL by
ACC to sel1 subordinated debentures to the public. 
ACC is the
parent of Lincoln Savings and Loan Association and a second
application was altowed to go effective by such counsel acting
under authority granted by the connissionar. Lincoln savings and
Loan Association has been sej.zed by federal regulators. 
ACC is
currentfy in bankruptcy proceedings. The purchasers and holders
of the subordinated debentures have initialed a crass action suit
AOM 5rl7


KATHRYN DONOVAN
against the State of California in
county because the securities have
Iittle 
vaIue.
-2-
January 31, 1990
the Superior court of Orange
no$/ been deterrnined to be of
The Commissioner did not deny the effectiveness of the
gualification of the ACC securities because of her inability to
f i.nd, on the basis of the then current information, that the
proposed plan of business of ACC or the proposed issuance or sale
of the subordinated debentures $ras not fair, just or eguj-table,
or that ACC did not intend to transact its business fairly 
and
honestly, or that the subordinated debentures proposed to be
issued or the method to be used in j.ssuing them lrould tend to
work a fraud upon the purchasers thereof, all as required by the
CSL. See Section 251-40 (a) .
Certain employees of the Department were involved in rnaking
recomnendations with respect to the ACC application. 
These and
other employees have al-so been involved, in their official-
capacity, in activities after ACC dectared bankruptcy, including
testimony before legislative conmittees. The Commissioner and
various members of her staff have testified periodically before
committees and subcornittees of the California Legisfature as welI
as Congress. The Comrnissioner and several- staff members of the
Department now have been subpoenaed for depositions in connection
with the pending class action La!'/suit.
The commissioner proposes to hire an independent, outside counsel-
from a ]aw firm to represent her and certain employees in
connection with the class action and any other actions which may
be brought j,n connection with the offer and saLe of the
subordinated debentures. Neither the Conmj-ssioner nor any member
of her staff, to the Commissionerts knowledge, will have a
trfinancial interestrt in the law firrn, as defined in section 87103
of the Political Refom Act.
OUESTIONS PRESENTED
l-. 
Does the relationship between the independent, outside
counsel and/or the 1aw firrn and the Departrnent present
any potential- conflict of interests, of which the
counsel and/or the law firrn should be aware, which
would require the counsel and/or the 1aw firrn to
disqualify itself from representing other clients
before the Department with respect to any matters?
2. 
Does the Political Reform Act require that the
Commissioner and those Department empl-oyees involved in
the Acc matter disqualify themselves frorn participating
in their official 
capacity in any matters involving the
independent, outside counsel and / or the Ia$/ firm,


KATHRYN DONOVAN
Assistant Cornmiss ioner
Office of Pol icy
(9L6) 322-3663
WK: nd
-3-
January 31, 1990
includinq individuats of that taw firm or groups of
individuals of that 1ar./ firm whose specialty or area of
practice invoLves matters subject to the jurisdiction of the
Department, but lrhich are not in any way connected by the
above-mentioned ACC legal action to be handled by the
independent, outside counsel and/ or the law firm?
As indicated above, due to the urgency of this matter, I an
requesting expedited treatment of this request. If I can be of
any assistance, please do not hesitate to contact me. In my
absence, please contact Robert M. Arhelger, Senior Corporations
Counsel , Office of Pol j,cy of this Department. Mr. Arhel-gerts
telephone number is (916) 322-3675.
,{./14 L'?
WTLLIAI'{ KENEFICK

sections.question:
1. Does the relationship between the independent, outside counsel and/or the law firm and the Department present any potential conflict of interests, of which the counsel and/or the law firm should be aware, which would require the counsel and/or the law firm to disqualify itself from representing other clients before the Department with respect to any matters?

2. Does the Political Reform Act require that the Commissioner and those Department employees involved in the ACC matter disqualify themselves from participating in their official capacity in any matters involving the independent, outside counsel and / or the law firm, including individuals of that law firm or groups of individuals of that law firm whose specialty or area of practice involves matters subject to the jurisdiction of the Department, but which are not in any way connected by the above-mentioned ACC legal action to be handled by the independent, outside counsel and/ or the law firm?

sections.question_synthetic: The requester asks whether hiring an independent law firm to represent the Commissioner and Department employees in a pending class action lawsuit presents conflicts of interest that would require the law firm to disqualify itself from other Department matters, and whether the Commissioner and staff must recuse themselves from matters involving that law firm unrelated to the ACC litigation.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: This is an incoming request letter dated January 31, 1990, not an FPPC response. Document shows OCR quality issues in header (Cclifornlc, Memorondum). No conclusion/advice from FPPC is present. Requester is William Kenefick requesting expedited treatment.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: 1. Does the relationship between the independent, outside counsel and/or the law firm and the Department present any potential conflict of interests, of which the counsel and/or the law firm should be aware, which would require the counsel and/or the law firm to disqualify itself from representing other clients before the Department with respect to any matters?

2. Does the Political Reform Act require that the Commissioner and those Department employees involved in the ACC matter disqualify themselves from participating in their official capacity in any matters involving the independent, outside counsel and / or the law firm, including individuals of that law firm or groups of individuals of that law firm whose specialty or area of practice involves matters subject to the jurisdiction of the Department, but which are not in any way connected by the above-mentioned ACC legal action to be handled by the independent, outside counsel and/ or the law firm?

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request from the California Department of Corporations seeking informal FPPC advice regarding potential conflicts of interest arising from the Commissioner's proposal to hire outside counsel to represent herself and Department employees in a class action lawsuit related to a controversial ACC securities approval decision.
